business.headline: Sensex drops over 500 points: Why is stock market falling today?Dalal Street fell sharply on Tuesday as an Asian market selloff and weak IT shares dragged benchmarks lower. The decline has shifted focus to US rate signals, monsoon worries and fading momentum after the recent rally.

Got your Form 16? Here's why Form 130 won't affect your ITR filing this yearConfused about Form 16 and Form 130? If you're preparing to file your ITR, here's why the upcoming change won't affect your return this year.

Iran wants to sell oil to India after US waiver. Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

Pension complaint? Here are 5 ways PFRDA's new AI-powered platform can help youThe Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority has launched a new AI-powered platform called PFRDA Pension Sahayak, aimed at making pension grievance redressal faster, simpler and more accessible.

Seoul's Kospi crashes 10%, Japan's Nikkei falls 4%. Why are Asian markets down?Asian markets tumbled as investors sold tech and AI stocks, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 10% and Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6%.

Expecting an ITR deadline extension? Here's why it may not happen this yearMany taxpayers are hoping for another extension to the ITR filing deadline, as seen in previous years. However, this year's filing season appears different, and the chances of additional time may be lower than expected.

India-US trade talks enter final stretch ahead of July 24 deadlineUnion Minister Piyush Goyal and US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer are meeting in New Delhi to finalise the first phase of the India-US trade pact. The talks are significant as both sides seek tariff gains before the July 24 deadline.

SpaceX gets a reality check days after historic IPO listing, loses $600 billionLess than two weeks after making history, SpaceX is already confronting the burden of expectation. Investors have embraced Elon Musk's vision; now they want evidence that the business can deliver.

Sensex opens 170 points lower; Nifty tops 24,000; Infosys down 3%The S&P BSE Sensex fell 77.92 points, or 0.10%, to 77,016.15 in early trade, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 18.90 points, or 0.08%, to 24,083.35.

SpaceX loses $600bn in 3 days, nearly 3 times Ambani and Adani's combined wealthThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

Meta, CRED and Kunal Shah: What's behind the $900 million deal?Meta's investment in CRED and its founder Kunal Shah has renewed interest in the fintech company's journey, business model and what lies ahead for one of India's most closely watched startups.

Tata Electronics hit by cyberattack; 2 lakh files linked to Apple, Tesla leakedThe breach is the latest setback for Apple's supply chain in India, where Tata faces scrutiny over alleged contamination of farmlands near one of its iPhone parts plants, Reuters reported.

8th Pay Commission in Lucknow: How much could salaries and pensions increase?With the 8th Pay Commission holding fresh talks in Lucknow, expectations around pay revisions and pension increases are once again in focus. The discussions could help shape the recommendations that follow.

Silver jumps Rs 5,000, gold up Rs 2,000: Time to buy or sell?Silver and gold prices have surged sharply today, sparking fresh interest among investors after recent volatility. So is this rally a buying opportunity or time to book profits?

Sensex closes 291 points higher, Nifty above 24,100; RIL up 1%The S&P BSE Sensex rose 291.17 points, or 0.38%, to close at 77,094.07, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 89.80 points, or 0.37%, to settle at 24,102.90.

India's economy is booming. So why are foreign investors looking away?For years, India rolled out the red carpet for foreign investors, citing its vast market, consumer base and ease of doing business. Now, as global capital chases artificial intelligence and semiconductors, those strengths no longer seem enough.

8th Pay Commission: What pensioners and family pension beneficiaries can expectThe 8th Pay Commission has begun consultations in Lucknow with unions, associations and department representatives. Its recommendations could shape pensions, family pensions and retirement security for millions.

Can Calcutta Stock Exchange make a comeback? Bengal govt proposes revival planAfter years of uncertainty and legal hurdles, the Calcutta Stock Exchange has received a fresh lifeline from the Bengal government. Can the revival proposal help bring trading back to the exchange?

India wants lower tariffs in US trade deal; hopes to sign 1st phase before July 24India is pressing the United States for lower tariffs and better market access in the proposed trade pact. The talks also carry wider significance as both sides weigh sensitive sectors and broader strategic cooperation.

Old vs new tax regime: How many times can you change your choice?Old or new tax regime? It's a choice many taxpayers revisit every year. But before making the switch, it's important to know how often you're actually allowed to change your tax regime.

entertainment.headline: Just a reel: CM Vijay hits back at critics mocking his film-to-politics journey In the Tamil Nadu Assembly, Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay hit back at critics who dismissed him as a film star. The speech linked his political rise to years of fan club activism and sparked a walkout and viral reactions.

Shraddha Kapoor plays legendary artiste Vithabai in Eetha teaser The teaser of Eetha shows Shraddha Kapoor as Vithabai Bhau Mang Narayangaonkar preparing to perform just after childbirth. Directed by Laxman Utekar, the film is set to release in August this year.

Director RJ Balaji admits VFX flaws in Suriya's Karuppu, apologises to audiences RJ Balaji addressed criticism over Karuppu's VFX, apologised to audiences, and promised a better visual experience in his next directorial venture, during a recent interview.

Filmmaker Raj Nidimoru on how Samantha stayed relevant for years: She's self-made Raj Nidimoru said Samantha Ruth Prabhu has stayed relevant by constantly evolving her craft. His remarks, as Maa Inti Bangaaram opened strongly, spotlight longevity in an industry often unkind to women.

Prakash Raj faces non-bailable warrant over alleged multiple voter IDs A Bengaluru court has issued a non-bailable warrant against actor Prakash Raj. The order follows allegations that he holds voter identity cards in more than one state and raises the possibility of his arrest.

Alliance trailer: Kunal Kemmu hosts a high-stakes survival game of trust The trailer and contestant pairs for Kunal Kemmu-hosted upcoming reality show Alliance has been unveiled ahead of its June 26 premiere. The series turns real-life allies into rivals in a daily game driven by trust, strategy and survival.

Emraan Hashmi returns to horror with musical film Rooh, set for 2027 release Emraan Hashmi will headline Rooh, a musical-horror film directed by Mayank Sharma for a 2027 release. He is currently gearing up for the release of Awarapan 2.

Riteish's viral reaction to question on Salman Khan, Sanjay Dutt's 'lock-up' time Riteish Deshmukh shut down a reporter's question about inviting Salman Khan and Sanjay Dutt to Lock Upp: Sach Ya Sazaa, calling it unworthy of a response. The actor clarified that his role is limited to hosting the reality show and not selecting its contestants.

Ameesha Patel says Amrish Puri was a doll to work with on Gadar Ameesha Patel recalled working with Amrish Puri on Gadar: Ek Prem Katha and described him as a warm, witty co-star. She said his kindness and humour eased her nerves and left her with lasting memories from the set.

Heard Chai Ki Tapri yet? Latent 2's desi anthem is the most viral thing on internet A song popularly called the Chai Ki Tapri track from India's Got Latent Season 2 has gone viral online. Celebrity posts, judge reactions and fan-made reels have turned it into the show's breakout moment.

When Salman Khan told 19-year-old Dia Mirza she'd play his mother someday During Farah Khan's vlog, Dia Mirza recalled Salman Khan telling her she would one day play his mother on screen. The chat also revisited chaotic Tumko Na Bhool Paayenge shoots and the home Dia bought with her earnings from the film.

Lock Upp 2: Ram Kapoor, Shivangi Joshi, Pamela Serena among 14 confirmed contestants Actors Ram Kapoor, Shivangi Joshi and socialite Pamela Serena have been confirmed for Lock Upp: Sach ya Sazaa. Their entry comes as the Netflix season launches with new hosts and a reworked format.

Veteran actors Mallika Sukumaran, Lakshmipriya resign from Malayalam film body Malayalam actors Mallika Sukumaran and Lakshmipriya resigned from the Association of Malayalam Movie Artistes (AMMA) as the fallout from Shweta Menon's exit widened. Fresh calls for new leadership have sharpened the association's internal turmoil.

India's Got Latent 2 BTS: Alia Bhatt praises Samay Raina, shares Raha anecdote A behind-the-scenes clip from India's Got Latent 2 shows actor Alia Bhatt praising comedian Samay Raina and sharing a story about watching the show beside Raha. The video has added to the conversation around the new season, which has drawn mixed reactions online.

Jimmy Fallon slammed for hosting sexual assault-accused UFC star Conor McGregor Jimmy Fallon is facing backlash after hosting UFC star Conor McGregor on The Tonight Show. Social media users slammed the host for featuring McGregor and questioned why the interview did not address the civil case involving Nikita Hand.

I threw up: Actors Olivia, Ewan react to mother-son kiss from House of The Dragon 3 In House of the Dragon Season 3, Episode 1, Alicent tries to push Aemond towards Harrenhal after meeting Rhaenyra, but he kisses her instead. The moment unsettles the Green camp's power balance as the war for the Iron Throne intensifies.

Off Campus stars Mika Abdalla and Josh Heuston dating? Viral pics spark buzz Photos and videos from Paris have sparked fresh dating rumours around Off Campus co-stars Mika Abdalla and Josh Heuston. The speculation has grown amid Abdalla's recent split from actor Jake Short.

Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 review: TVF's most underrated gem just got betterRelease Date:Tue Jun 23, 2026 Gram Chikitsalay Season 2 review: Dr Prabhat Sinha returns to Bhatkandi as his underused health centre battles the local quack's popularity. Season 2 uses humour and chaos to explore rural healthcare, superstition and small-town dignity.

Why CM Vijay's birthday felt different this year, reveals actor Keerthy Suresh Keerthy Suresh said CM Vijay's birthday felt different this year, with no teaser or trailer to mark the day. Her note framed his first birthday as Tamil Nadu Chief Minister as a shift from screen image to public service.

general news.headline: Is Pakistan using Indus Valley to trick world on Indus treaty shelved by India?Pakistan is embracing the Indus Valley Civilisation as part of its national identity in a big way. It's a notable shift for a country that held the Sindh conquest of Muhammad bin Qasim in 712 CE as its foundational moment. So, why is Pakistan suddenly in love with Harappa and Mohenjo-daro? Does it have to do with the Indus Waters Treaty, which India has kept in abeyance?

Pune man, who died while taking pic on fort, pushed by fiancee, her lover: CopsKetan Vishal Agarwal's death at Pune's Lohagad Fort, initially believed to be an accidental fall, is now being investigated as a suspected murder. Police suspect his fiancee and another man may have conspired to push him into the valley.

Kunal Shah is new WhatsApp CEO, people can't stop talking about his college degreeMeta has named CRED founder Kunal Shah to lead WhatsApp globally. The move has also revived discussion about his Philosophy degree and unfinished MBA.

Dust storm engulfs Delhi-NCR: Red alert issued, light rain to followWith the monsoon gradually advancing northward, weather experts say such intense dust storms and thunderstorms may continue to occur across northwestern India in the coming days before more widespread rainfall activity takes over.

We conducted a fuel efficiency test of E85. Here's what we foundWith E85 fuel priced nearly Rs 20 lower per litre than E20, it appears to be a bargain. However, ethanol's lower energy density also means higher fuel consumption. So, does the lower pump price actually translate into lower running costs? We put the Suzuki Gixxer SF flex fuel through a mileage test to find out.

Seoul's Kospi crashes 10%, Japan's Nikkei falls 4%. Why are Asian markets down?Asian markets tumbled as investors sold tech and AI stocks, with South Korea's Kospi plunging 10% and Japan's Nikkei falling 3.6%.

3 CBSE toppers from Jharkhand; Class 12 re-evaluation result rewrites merit listCBSE's Class 12 re-evaluation results pushed Jharkhand students to the top of Commerce, Science and Arts. The revised scores gave the state a first across all three streams and included Avani Kejriwal's perfect 500.

Tankers are nearby, but will India buy?Iran has begun pitching crude oil to Indian and other Asian refiners after the US waived sanctions for 60 days amid peace-deal negotiations. With Iranian tankers close to Indian shores, New Delhi appears to be an attractive market for Tehran, but will Indian refiners return to buying Iranian oil?

I tried a micro-retirement, and it changed how I think about workAfter six years of continuous work, I took a one-year micro-retirement to recharge and reassess my priorities. The break taught me the importance of work-life balance, lifelong learning, good health, and valuing time. More than a career pause, it changed how I approach work, relationships, and life.

NTA releases CUET UG 2026 results, female participation jumps 20%The National Testing Agency has declared the CUET UG 2026 results, allowing candidates to download scorecards and move to the admissions stage across participating universities.

Rishabh Pant takes huge pay cut for Delhi Capitals return, Kuldeep joins LSGIPL 2027: Lucknow Super Giants have mutually parted ways with Rishabh Pant after a disastrous Indian Premier League season. Pant is set to return to his former franchise Delhi Capitals, having taken a huge pay cut.

Lucknow fire revives debate on smart locks turning buildings into trapsRecent fires in Lucknow, Delhi and Indore have raised questions about whether smart locks and sealed exits trapped residents inside. The cases have sharpened concern that security systems without fail-safe evacuation measures can become hazards in emergencies.

Once Hormuz reopens, can Pakistan hit India's Basmati feast in the Middle East?The reopening of the Strait of Hormuz might give Pakistani Basmati exporters an opportunity to grab some market share from India in the Middle East. Pakistani Basmati exporters now have a subsidy-led price advantage. Can it dent India's Basmati sales in the Middle East in the long-term?

Idli, Indonesia and India: The 1000-year mystery behind a breakfast favouriteSomewhere in a news article, you may have read about the origin of the idli and its long association not with India, but with Indonesia. The moment such a claim is mentioned, it often raises eyebrows. So, what is the story of Idli? Let's explore it with this article.

Seven PMs in UK in 10 years. Why British PM's fail to keep their jobSince 2016, the UK will be seeing a seventh prime minister check into 10, Downing Street. As Keir Starmer leaves in less than 2 years, here's why recent British PMs fail to remain in office.

Prakash Raj faces non-bailable warrant over alleged multiple voter IDsA Bengaluru court has issued a non-bailable warrant against actor Prakash Raj. The order follows allegations that he holds voter identity cards in more than one state and raises the possibility of his arrest.

From wedding preparations to tragic end: Engaged couple dies in Lucknow fireA fire at a Lucknow commercial building killed 15 people, including a couple set to marry. The blaze turned wedding preparations into mourning and exposed the human toll of the tragedy.

FIFA World Cup: Messi breaks records. The spell remains unbrokenFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's latest World Cup record is another milestone in a career overflowing with them. Yet the enduring story is not the number itself, but the feeling of watching a footballing genius still at work.

'Tukde tukde' forces at Jantar Mantar, Rahul spreading fear: Dharmendra PradhanUnion Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan said NEET is likely to become a computer-based test from next year as the government works to prevent paper leaks and strengthen exam security. He also said efforts are underway to dismantle the paper mafia and ensure students face fewer disruptions during examinations.

From 95% to perfect 100% in CBSE: Ranchi girl gets 24 extra marks after re-evaluationA 24-mark boost after CBSE re-evaluation transformed Ranchi student Avni Kejriwal's Class 12 result from 95.2% to a perfect 100%. The dramatic revision underscores the importance of the board's answer-sheet review mechanism for students seeking score corrections.

Season 2 uses humour and chaos to explore rural healthcare, superstition and small-town dignity.

iPhone 18 Pro, 18 Pro Max launch soon: How much they are likely to cost in IndiaApple's upcoming iPhone 18 Pro series is likely to launch soon, but pricing in India could see a rise this time, with analysts telling India Today Tech that a possible $100–$150 (around Rs 9,500–Rs 14,200) increase could be on the cards. Rising component costs and currency pressure could force Apple to revise prices after years of relative stability.

Girl, 11, kidnapped from Delhi footpath, raped and murdered in Gurugram forestPolice traced and arrested the accused after scanning hundreds of CCTV cameras, and said he confessed to the horrific crime during interrogation.

SpaceX loses $600bn in 3 days, nearly 3 times Ambani and Adani's combined wealthThe wipeout is nearly three times the combined wealth of India's two richest businessmen — Reliance Industries Chairman Mukesh Ambani, whose net worth stands at $88.3 billion, and Adani Group Chairman Gautam Adani, whose fortune is estimated at $120 billion.

sports.headline: Sree Charani becomes No. 1 T20I bowler a year after making international debut Sree Charani became the world's top-ranked T20I bowler just over a year after her India debut. The left-arm spinner has been in fine form in the ongoing Women's T20 World Cup campaign in England.

Argentina make life easier for Messi. Portugal make it harder for Ronaldo Argentina have built a World Cup-winning ecosystem around Lionel Messi, allowing him to thrive at 39. Portugal, despite possessing immense talent, are still searching for the right formula to maximise Cristiano Ronaldo as another crucial test awaits against Uzbekistan.

Promising Suryansh Shedge replaces Nitish Reddy for Ireland, England T20I series Suryansh Shedge has replaced injured Nitish Kumar Reddy in India's T20I squad for the Ireland and England series. The Mumbai all-rounder earns a maiden call-up after strong India A and domestic performances.

Rishabh Pant takes huge pay cut for Delhi Capitals return, Kuldeep joins LSG IPL 2027: Lucknow Super Giants have mutually parted ways with Rishabh Pant after a disastrous Indian Premier League season. Pant is set to return to his former franchise Delhi Capitals, having taken a huge pay cut.

From 1 to 100: Messi fans across generations steal the spotlight in World Cup Lionel Messi fans from two very different generations stole the spotlight on Monday, when Argentina took the field against Austria in the FIFA World Cup. A 100-year-old grandma, and a child, barely one-year-old, were seen celebrating Messi and Argentina in their group-stage game.

ODI queens, T20 learners: South Africa hold up the mirror to Harmanpreet's India South Africa delivered a timely reality check to India in Manchester. The six-wicket defeat exposed glaring shortcomings in Harmanpreet Kaur's side and raised fresh questions about their ability to translate ODI dominance into T20 success as the battle for the semi-finals heats up.

After Hardik Pandya, all-rounder Nitish Reddy ruled out of UK tour India have been dealt a big blow ahead of their tour of the United Kingdom. All-rounder Nitish Reddy has been ruled out of the Ireland and England series due to a quadriceps injury.

Birth certificate says 15, big-match record says Sooryavanshi loves pressure Teenage prodigies arrive regularly in cricket. What sets Vaibhav Sooryavanshi apart, at least for now, is not just his talent but his growing habit of producing defining performances when the pressure peaks.

Dream fulfilled: Sooryavanshi speechless after seeing his name on India jersey India's teenage batting star Vaibhav Sooryavanshi said he was left overwhelmed after seeing his name on an India jersey for the first time, calling it the fulfilment of a dream he had cherished since he first picked up a cricket bat.

Messi beware: France coach backs Mbappe to claim FIFA World Cup scoring crown France manager Didier Deschamps backed Kylian Mbappe to eventually surpass Lionel Messi and become the FIFA World Cup's all-time leading scorer after the France captain struck twice in a 3-0 win over Iraq on Monday.

Viking row: After taking over Times Square, Norway fans celebrate with Haaland Erling Haaland and Norway's players performed the Viking row with supporters in New York after beating Senegal 3-2. Norway qualified from Group I alongside France into the knockouts round.

World Cup 2026: Haaland brace sends Norway into knockouts with 3-2 win over SenegalErling Haaland continued his prolific run at the FIFA World Cup 2026, scoring twice as Norway beat Senegal 3-2 in a thrilling Group I contest on Tuesday, June 23 (IST) to secure qualification for the Round of 32.

FIFA World Cup: Messi breaks records. The spell remains unbrokenFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's latest World Cup record is another milestone in a career overflowing with them. Yet the enduring story is not the number itself, but the feeling of watching a footballing genius still at work.

Barcelona target Julian Alvarez wants to leave Atletico Madrid to fulfil his dreamArgentina forward Julian Alvarez has confirmed he wants to leave Atletico Madrid, saying a transfer would help him "fulfil his dream", amid interest from Barcelona, Paris Saint-Germain and Real Madrid.

Mbappe keeps Messi on his toes as France crush Iraq in weather-hit clashKylian Mbappe scored twice as France beat Iraq 3-0 in a World Cup match interrupted by severe weather. The win sent France into the Round of 32 and kept Mbappe within two World Cup goals of Lionel Messi.

France vs Iraq delayed by rain, becomes 1st World Cup match affected by weatherFrance's World Cup game against Iraq was delayed at half-time after thunderstorms closed in on Philadelphia. This was the first time during the tournament that match had to be halted due to adverse weather conditions.

Days after India A's tri-series win, Tilak Varma lights up TG20 with 136 off 56 ballsTilak Varma drove Medak Falcons past Warangal Warriors with an unbeaten 136 in a chase of 259 just days after India A's tri-series win in Sri Lanka. The match turned out to be a run-fest featuring Aman Rao Perala's 142.

FIFA's Messi bias again? Fans spot ignored foul before Argentine's record goalFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi's record-breaking night against Austria should have been remembered purely for history. Instead, a controversial foul in the build-up to his landmark World Cup goal reignited fan claims of favourable officiating towards Argentina and their captain.

Jordan 1-2 Algeria Highlights: Algeria come from behind to knock Jordan outFIFA World Cup, Jordan vs Algeria Highlights: Algeria have taken the lead with another lovely goal from a corner. Striker Al Gouiri heads in the ball past Abulaila and Jordan are on the brink of getting knocked out of the tournament.

The inevitable Messi-ah breaks World Cup record to send Argentina into knockoutsFIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi missed an early penalty but still ended the night with two goals and another World Cup record. The Argentina captain became the tournament's all-time leading scorer as the reigning champions booked their place in the knockout rounds.

Messi becomes World Cup's top goalscorer, shatters multiple records on the wayLionel Messi put Argentina ahead against Austria and became the World Cup's all-time top goalscorer. The 38th-minute strike pushed him past Miroslav Klose and added another layer to his record-laden career.

England name squad for T20I series against India, uncapped all-rounder called upEngland have named a 17-player squad for the T20I series against India, with James Coles receiving his first call-up. The selection also brings back Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood while injuries have ruled out Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton.

Marketa Vondrousova hit with 4-year ban over doping test fiascoFormer Wimbledon champion Marketa Vondrousova has been banned for four years after an independent tribunal ruled against her in an anti-doping test refusal case. The ITIA charged her for allegedly denying a doping officer entry to her home in December 2025.

ARG vs AUT Highlights: Messi double puts Argentina into Round of 32Argentina (ARG) vs Austria (AUT) Highlights, FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi breaks the World Cup record and bags a brace as Argentina move into the Round of 32 with their 2-0 win against Austria.

Suryakumar Yadav draws parallels with Men's T20 WC win to inspire India WomenWomen's T20 World Cup 2026, INDW vs SAW: Suryakumar Yadav inspired the India Women's team by recalling India Men's turnaround in the T20 World Cup after a loss to South Africa.

Germany dealt major setback as Schlotterbeck ruled out for rest of FIFA World CupGermany's FIFA World Cup campaign has suffered a major blow with defender Nico Schlotterbeck ruled out for the rest of the tournament and several months after sustaining a left ankle ligament injury during the 2-1 win over Ivory Coast.

MS Dhoni is Roger Federer of cricket, Virat Kohli mirrors Carlos Alcaraz: Sanju SamsonSanju Samson compared MS Dhoni to Roger Federer for his calm, tactical brilliance and Virat Kohli to Carlos Alcaraz for his explosive, high-intensity style. Alcaraz, a two-time Wimbledon champion, is set to miss the 2026 edition due to fitness concerns.

Gurpreet Sandhu on India missing FIFA World Cup: Why didn't we reach Asian Cup?Gurpreet Singh Sandhu sparked debate by saying India's World Cup absence reflects a deeper issue, failure to consistently qualify for and progress in the AFC Asian Cup, the key stepping stone in Asian football.

Ronaldo hat-trick against Uzbekistan? Michael Owen slams critics of Portugal starFIFA World Cup 2026: Michael Owen defended Cristiano Ronaldo after Portugal's draw with DR Congo and backed him to answer critics against Uzbekistan. His remarks come amid scrutiny over Ronaldo's form, Portugal's balance and Roberto Martínez's authority.

I'm a failure as captain: Chamari Athapaththu after Sri Lanka face early exit threatChamari Athapaththu was left emotional as Sri Lanka suffered a five-wicket defeat to the West Indies, leaving their Women's T20 World Cup campaign on the brink of an early exit. Athapaththu said she felt like a 'failure as captain' for not guiding Sri Lanka to a World Cup semi.

Motherhood and cricket can coexist, says ICC in landmark moveThe ICC has launched its Return to Play Post-Pregnancy Guidelines to support women cricketers returning to elite sport after childbirth through a structured, science-backed framework.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
